mission.northStar: Kuma — your AI Software Healing Engineer. Software as a living organism, not a pile of scripts.
mission.statement: An AI code-review service that reviews every pull request with six specialist agents, verifies each finding against the real diff, and proposes human-approved fixes. The wedge product is live; the platform ambitions (telemetry, proactive improvement) are deliberately deferred.

principles.title: Never fabricate
principles.body: Never fabricate data, status, or a passing result. Empty states must say what is actually true. A control that cannot act must be `disabled` — never a live-looking button with no handler. If you cannot verify something, say so.

principles.title: The approval gate is the moat
principles.body: Never adopt auto-actions that bypass human approval or CI. Kuma proposes; humans decide. No agent message is consent.

principles.title: Transparency earns the right to propose
principles.body: A review is not a verdict handed down from nowhere. Transparency is not a dashboard afterthought here — it is how the system earns the right to propose changes to your code.

principles.title: null is not []
principles.body: `null` means this surface is unavailable to you. `[]` means you have zero errors. Returning `[]` on a gate failure tells the user a comforting lie. Never let an access outcome masquerade as a data outcome.

principles.title: One resolver, one truth
principles.body: No surface may re-derive a security-gated fact locally. For a security gate, two copies are two places to drift.

principles.title: A badge is a promise
principles.body: A `soon` or `partial` badge promises the reader that the control below it will not do anything surprising. Pair it with a `disabled` control — a badge on a live-looking button is worse than no badge at all.

principles.title: Green units alone are not done
principles.body: Verify by driving the real flow, not just a green test. Several features passed unit tests but were never confirmed end to end. Done means observed working.

principles.title: Evidence first, ticks after
principles.body: Never accept a checkbox as evidence of the check. The definition-of-done run produces a file before it produces checkmarks. Self-reported verification is not verification.

principles.title: A stale record is worse than none
principles.body: A closed item presented as the top live security gap is worse than no backlog — it misdirects whoever plans next. A record claiming an open hole that no longer exists misstates the security posture of shipped code.

principles.title: Contradicting the plan is the most valuable report
principles.body: Anything you discovered that contradicts the plan — this is the most valuable thing you can report, and it is never unwelcome.

principles.title: Explain surprises, do not symptom-fix
principles.body: Log unexplained anomalies; do not symptom-fix on the critical path. If a result surprises you, explain it to reproduction-level confidence before moving on.

programmes.label: Product build 1.1 – 1.6
programmes.caveat: All six shipped by 2026-07-14. These numbers are historical and share no meaning with the P-numbers or the observability phases.

programmes.label: SuperLog capability roadmap P1 – P5
programmes.caveat: STALE — §6 of the build-status map records three items this roadmap still lists as unstarted that are in fact done. Correct it before planning off it.

phases.label: Finish the review → action loop
phases.evidence: retry worker started in 2ea559d; confidence column and prUrl still open

phases.label: Tenant telemetry ingestion platform
phases.evidence: deferred by design — scope, not a gap

programmes.label: Observability initiative 0 – 4
programmes.caveat: This is the numbering docs/roadmap/backlog.md is organised around. Phases 0–4 shipped; 2b holds what Phase 2 deferred and is partly open.

phases.label: Trustworthy gates
phases.evidence: closed with two criteria unmet; both finally closed in Phase 4

phases.label: Credential integrity, frontend consumption, harness efficiency
Trustworthy CI signal + harness hardening
phases.evidence: 8 items deferred out of it, all still open

programmes.label: Orchestration waves A – C
programmes.caveat: Phase A shipped as @arete/orchestration and is consumed by lib/issue-pipeline/driver.ts. B and C were deferred, not cancelled.

items.title: AI model connections
items.works: Connect, reconnect, set-active, disconnect and remove key. Ollama auto-detect with streamed model pull. Real diagnostics on failure.

items.title: Review history
items.works: Server-filtered risk tabs, real review rows, pagination, honest empty state.

items.title: Incidents list
items.works: Open / Resolved / Noise / All tabs over real incidents. New investigation creates a real Incident row.

items.title: Settings & billing
items.works: GitHub OAuth connect, real billing usage against the free-tier limit. No self-serve upgrade — stated plainly rather than faked.
items.gap: No self-serve upgrade path exists. Tracked separately as stripe-pricing-tiers.

items.title: Overview
items.works: Stat tiles, Sensorium code map, setup checklist derived from real DB facts, preset tabs, time range.
items.gap: Two checklist sub-steps (coding-agent setup prompt, MCP Kuma) are not built. Planned telemetry connectors are inert.

items.title: Agents
items.works: Per-agent chat is genuinely live against the connected model, with provider errors surfaced honestly. Findings come from real reviews.
items.gap: Every configuration control is local-only and never saved. The page's future is undecided — the spec locks Agents being absorbed into Services, and a commit did the reverse.

items.title: Services
items.works: Repo rail, live synthesizer console over SSE, status board, Scan, Fix it and Dismiss all hit real endpoints. Decomposed 1,346 → 459 lines with characterization tests first.
items.gap: The PR-send workflow was made reachable on main (1192d37) but that work is not on this branch. Re-verify after the rebase rather than trusting this row.

items.title: Incident detail
items.works: Mark and unmark noise, view a linked fix run, scrubbed alert payload tables — and opening an investigation now starts a fix drive, the same way a critical alert does.
items.gap: The fix run it starts halts at the human approve-and-send gate. That gate became reachable on main; this branch does not have it yet.

provenance.note: auto-start on creation was the user's explicit choice over a passive 'Fix it' button

items.title: Data connectors
items.works: GitHub App install, Stripe API key, and PostHog + Vercel OAuth.
items.gap: Every other connector is disabled pending its integration being published. Sentry is fully built and tested but gated on Sentry's own approval, not on us.
blockedBy: ext:Sentry integration approval is Sentry's decision, not ours

items.title: Search & notifications
items.works: Nothing — the breadcrumb is the only working part of the top bar.
items.gap: Command palette and notifications are both disabled placeholders.

items.title: Approval gate (human-in-the-loop)
items.works: Complete end to end: the approval record, the claim-and-enqueue handler, a running worker, and the apply call into the agents service.
items.gap: STALE ROW — an approvals panel with a genuinely new reject capability landed on main in a21f956, and the two Services HITL gates were made reachable in 1192d37. Neither is on this branch. Re-verify after rebasing; do not plan from this row as written.

items.title: Outbound webhooks
items.works: Signing, retry backoff, SSRF-guarded delivery and a delivery log — it fires on every persisted review, the retry worker now actually runs, and the management API is mounted behind session auth (list, register, enable/disable).
items.gap: No Settings screen calls it, so registering a destination still needs a direct API call. Whoever builds it: the whsec_ signing secret is returned exactly once, on create — no route can read it back, so the UI must show it once and say so.

items.title: Agent memory
items.works: Kuma really does record what it learns per repository and injects it into later reviews — and at the 20-row cap it now archives the oldest instead of refusing to learn.
items.gap: Nothing displays it. There is no screen for what Kuma has learned about a repository, or for retiring a memory that has gone stale.

items.title: Live throughput metrics
items.works: The worker publishes review-lifecycle metrics to a server-sent stream.
items.gap: No consumer. The source comments call it a dark wire.

items.title: Webhook delivery retries
items.works: The retry worker now runs — it previously had no caller at all, which was silent data loss. A failed delivery is re-attempted on an exponential backoff and rescheduled, verified against real Postgres.
items.gap: Nothing surfaces delivery health: nobody can see that a delivery failed, how many attempts remain, or the last error. That needs the webhook endpoints UI.

items.title: Tracing & log search
items.works: Full OpenTelemetry instrumentation now flows into ClickHouse and Jaeger, with trace deep-links.
items.gap: Exactly one metric reaches the UI. No trace view, no span drill-down, no log search.

items.title: Finding confidence
items.works: Confidence is computed and shown on scan and fix work items.
items.gap: Pull-request findings have no confidence column at all, so reviews show none. Needs a ReviewComment schema change.

items.title: Noise & escalation controls
items.works: A full state machine exists — silenced, under observation, escalated — with occurrence thresholds.
items.gap: A silence / un-silence control was in flight in another lane at the time of this audit. Re-verify before building anything on top of this row.

items.title: Telemetry freshness
items.works: The grid reads a real telemetry snapshot per repository, and the UI states its age honestly rather than implying it is live.
items.gap: Snapshots are only captured during a review and there is no background poller, so a quiet repo shows arbitrarily stale data.

items.title: MCP servers
items.works: Kuma can consume third-party MCP servers, driven from the CLI. OAuth tokens are encrypted at rest under ARETE_MCP_TOKEN_KEY, and the credential store is gitignored.
items.gap: No dashboard surface to add or list them — connecting a server is still a CLI-only operation.

items.title: Slack / Linear / PagerDuty relays
items.gap: Blocked on outbound webhook management. Once that lands, each relay is a thin consumer of the Phase-1 webhooks — no bespoke plumbing.

items.title: Public API & API keys
items.gap: No key issuance, no read or management REST surface. Keys must be scoped, hashed at rest, shown once, and revocable.

items.title: Review scope filters
items.gap: No way to say which paths Kuma should or should not review. Filtering must be server-side — scope cannot be trusted from the client.

items.title: Kuma as an MCP server
items.gap: Expose review tools (get_review, list_findings, request_approval) over MCP so coding agents pull Kuma's judgement. The roadmap calls this more valuable than Kuma consuming other MCP servers. Only the client half exists.

items.title: Tenant telemetry ingestion
items.gap: Ingest endpoints, ingest keys, alert rules and a service map. Deliberately deferred — the current stack observes Kuma itself, not your services. Do not adopt per-span metered billing.

items.title: GitLab MR diff fetch + comment posting
items.gap: Dispatched 2026-07-10 to branch feat/webhook-gitlab-complete. That branch does not exist in git and no closeout, status doc or backlog entry ever mentions it again. GitLab is listed as shipped in the 07-14 inventory, so something landed — establish what actually exists before rebuilding.

items.title: Work-floor agent roster (PM-agent dispatching specialists)
items.gap: A problem enters, a PM-agent triages and dispatches specialists (reproduction, root-cause, fix-author, test-author, security, reviewer, QA), they message each other through the PM in a star topology, a QA agent validates the fix really works and routes failures back for another pass. Today orchestrator.py route() is a static cartesian fan-out with no triage step, a fixed review-only roster of six, and no inter-agent communication — none of them reproduces, root-causes or writes a fix.

provenance.note: Design-only. Presented for PM approval before any build; approval never recorded.

items.title: Docker sandbox workspace agent — run the PR's tests for real
items.gap: Of roughly 380 symbols declared across 48 plans, workspace.py is the one that is genuinely absent. Without it no agent can execute a customer's test suite, which is what gates the whole work-floor roster.

provenance.note: That roadmap exists only in the ridley worktree, not in pyrosome.

items.title: Glass Box cockpit in production
items.gap: 35 KB of design sitting idle, awaiting human approval before any feature-depth build. Today it is a dev-only sidecar on :4517 and returns null in production. Structurally undeployable without an installationId tenancy filter. The design reads agent narration off BullMQ QueueEvents with zero producer changes and uses OTel LangGraph spans as node-transition ground truth.

items.title: Give the fix pipeline a tool loop
items.gap: fix_pipeline.py makes one direct LLM call for a JSON blob. It should become a bounded tool loop reusing agents/base.py, publishing report_findings (required before any terminal tool), ask_human, propose_pr, resolve_incident and report_external_cause. Until it exists, the spec's rule that tool descriptions ARE the prompt cannot apply to healing at all.

items.title: Investigations surface (append-only event feed)
items.gap: Proposed, awaiting user review. An append-only InvestigationEvent model with a monotonic seq, a pure React-free buildActivityFeed projection that stores but never renders agent thinking, and SSE resume by seq — a client disconnect must never cancel the run, called out as the most common way resumable agent UIs break.

items.title: Decide: Agents absorbed into Services, or kept separate
items.gap: The spec locks 'the Agents page is absorbed into Services — one place to see what Kuma is doing to my services rather than two'. Commit 73e2040 did the reverse, putting the Services work-item inbox into the Agents rail and entrenching two pages. Recorded honestly: the lane brief's shorthand was ambiguous, so the wrong direction was baked into every option and the clarifying question could not catch it. Not reverted, not subsumed, not decided. The user's later refinement: agents should be a layer within Services — select the working agents, which opens their conversation and their parameter controls.

items.title: "What Kuma has learned" — agent memory view
items.gap: Zero dashboard references to AgentMemory. Needs a per-repository view of what Kuma learned, plus a way to retire a memory that has gone stale.

items.title: Consume /metrics/stream — the dark wire
items.gap: Mounted at server.ts:428 and self-described as a dark wire. Intended home is an Overview live-throughput tile, which needs a server-side proxy carrying the internal token.

items.title: MCP servers dashboard surface
items.gap: No way to add or list MCP servers from the product; it is CLI-only.

items.title: Delete synth-ledger.tsx — exported, imported by nothing
items.gap: The whole file has zero importers and carries a 'Backend gate coming soon' placeholder. The reachability roadmap says delete it.

items.title: Connect Workspace onboarding
items.gap: Clone-repo / select-folder, MCP-driven setup runnable from an agent, AI-model connect then Kuma install (skills + code mapping). Design constraint recorded at the time: extend the existing setup, do not greenfield — reconcile with GitHub App connect, model connections, Sensorium indexing and skills/loader. New checklist steps need real getAccountState signals, not fabricated checkmarks. Named as immediate-next on 2026-07-20 and inherited by no later document; the only surviving trace is two 'Coming soon' sub-steps.

items.title: Promote-by-id — PATCH /api/model-connections/:id/activate
items.gap: Bump a timestamp or add an explicit active column so api-key providers can be switched without re-entering the key, retiring the delete-and-recreate interim. An Engineer1↔Engineer2 cross-lane contract that was agreed and never built.

items.title: Change the Anthropic connect-UI default to a faster tier
items.gap: The connect UI defaults to the largest model, so every new connection is slow by default. Roughly a one-line change; named as immediate-next on 2026-07-20 and never picked up again.

items.title: Telemetry ingestion worker + snapshot history
items.gap: A background poller into TelemetrySnapshotRecord, keeping history rather than only the latest, retiring the producerless ClickHouse pulse. Deliberately not built unilaterally in this session: polling third-party APIs on a timer spends the customer's own quota, which is an outward-facing behaviour change and the user's call. The UI already states snapshot age honestly, so nothing is currently lying.

provenance.session: 2026-07-22 pyrosome — user chose to skip rather than defer silently

items.title: SDLC hardening — CI for four packages, and a deploy target
items.gap: orchestration, topology, net-guard and db have NO CI jobs at all. There is also no deploy pipeline and no hosting target. Named as immediate-next on 2026-07-20 and inherited by no later document.

items.title: Stripe pricing tiers + self-serve upgrade
items.gap: planTier is dead code; only a binary active / 50-free gate exists. Settings states the absence honestly rather than faking a button, so nothing is lying — but no later document revisits it.

items.title: Password reset + email verification
items.gap: User.emailVerified exists and is never written. A locked-out user has no recovery path at all.

items.title: lib/trends.ts buckets by local day while ClickHouse windows are UTC
items.gap: Every trend chart is off by one for any viewer whose timezone crosses midnight relative to UTC. Found during Stage 1 and deliberately not fixed, because fixing it shifts every chart in the product. The same class of bug made errors.test.ts fail for half of every day until 26fc554.

items.title: The send route never advances the work item to posted
items.gap: approve moves fixing → staged; after a successful send the container becomes posted while its work item stays staged. Invisible locally because STAGING_SERVICE_URL is unset.

items.title: Unguarded second checks.update inside the publish-failure catch
items.gap: On BOTH worker paths (worker.ts:134-144 and 239-249), if that call throws the job re-throws and BullMQ retries the whole pipeline — exactly the double-retry the two-block split was built to prevent. A whole review re-runs, at full LLM cost.

items.title: sse-handler.ts has no auth, no tenant scoping, CORS *
items.gap: Needs hardening before any non-local deploy. Harmless while everything is loopback; a deploy without this is a live data-exposure hole.

items.title: Prose credentials still reach sinks
items.gap: `password: hunter2` as free text matches neither a secret-value shape nor a blocklisted object key, and there is no URL to strip. Verified stored verbatim. Fixing it means amending the FROZEN §5 pattern set, with real false-positive risk — 'the password field is required' would be mangled. Applies to every sink.

items.title: .claude/ade-coordination.md has forked between worktrees
items.gap: Lines 1–341 are identical in pyrosome and ridley; after that each copy holds only its own lane's claims. pyrosome cannot see ridley's still-unretired exclusive claim on the whole components/dashboard/services/ folder. The file that exists to prevent collisions cannot be read completely from any single checkout. Whoever reconciles branches should union the tails, not overwrite one with the other.

items.title: Review LLM concurrency is unbounded per review
items.gap: orchestrator.py:353-381 fans out one LangGraph Send per (file × agent) with no max_concurrency, so a 20-file PR × 6 agents is roughly 120 concurrent LLM calls for one review — times the queue's 5, about 600 simultaneous provider calls. Measured and filed with evidence, deliberately unfixed.
blockedBy: ext:tuning needs a funded Anthropic key to measure against

items.title: Webhook-side fix failures never reach the counters
items.gap: no_repo and no_model terminate before Python is reached, so they never appear in arete.fix.* — a fix drive dying before dispatch is invisible in the metrics.

items.title: Running containers drift from compose on security settings
items.gap: Alertmanager served 0.0.0.0:9093 for hours after compose had been changed to 127.0.0.1:9093 — a container does not re-read its port mapping on restart. Third stale-infrastructure incident of the project. Proposal: make `docker compose up -d` a precondition of any verification that claims to test infra config.

items.title: A second Prometheus rules file needs a compose/config edit
items.gap: rule_files deliberately names one file rather than globbing, because a glob also matches the promtool test file, whose schema makes Prometheus reject the ENTIRE config. Anyone adding a second rules file must edit compose too.

items.title: MCP RFC 8414 discovery + dynamic client registration
items.gap: Never shipped. Server endpoints must be configured by hand.

items.title: expires_in as a numeric string silently stores expires_at = None
items.gap: A provider returning expires_in as "3600" rather than 3600 leaves expires_at unset, and the token is then treated as never expiring — so it is presented long after it is dead, and the failure surfaces as an opaque 401 from someone else's API.

items.title: _save_config mkdir does not retighten a pre-existing .agents/
items.gap: exist_ok=True means an upgrade from a version that created the directory world-readable leaves it that way. The file inside is 0o600, so this is defence in depth, not an open hole.

items.title: Token-endpoint errors embed up to 500 chars of raw response body
items.gap: Attacker-influenced content lands in an exception message that may be logged. Bounded, but unredacted.

items.title: review.publish child span absent on the check_run path
items.gap: processGitHubCheckRun emits only a review.run root with no children, so the two entry paths are not comparable in a trace.

items.title: Prometheus instance label derives from a per-process uuid4
items.gap: resource.ts:17-18 and observability.py:188 mint a fresh uuid per process, so every restart creates a new time series — unbounded cardinality growth in a long-running deployment.

items.title: otel-collector-config.yaml commits plaintext ClickHouse credentials
items.gap: Default local credentials committed to the repo. Harmless locally, a real leak the moment the same file is used anywhere else.

items.title: Decide how Python stamps issue fingerprints without a second copy
items.gap: Four record_exception sites in packages/agents are unstamped. Python's API accepts attributes=, so the mechanical half is trivial; the blocker is the value — it needs nine ordered regexes ported, and the contract forbids a second implementation because a drifting copy would split arete-agents errors into two groups with no test able to catch it from either side. Three options recorded: a shared normalization service, a generated port with a golden-vector fixture asserted from both languages, or an explicit spec amendment accepting the copy. Deliberately not decided silently.

items.title: The pino log path carries no issue fingerprint
items.gap: Two ClickHouse materialized views read LogAttributes['superlog.issue_fingerprint']; only the span lane is stamped, so those views are systematically incomplete. Called out as follow-up rather than left half-done.

items.title: Spec §6 gate 4 — exporter egress compliance has no owner
items.gap: Nothing routes exporter egress through @arete/net-guard. Either the gate is satisfied because exporters only talk to a loopback collector — in which case say so explicitly — or it is unmet and needs an owner. It is currently neither.

items.title: LRU memory eviction (deferred, not rejected)
items.gap: FIFO shipped as the user's explicit choice. LRU needs a lastCitedAt column plus read-path instrumentation in the shared @arete/db lane, and can layer on later without undoing the FIFO work.

items.title: chat-handler.test.ts carries the same cold-build timeout signature
items.gap: 4560ms file, 3 resetModules calls, 8 dynamic imports — the same shape that made tenancy and memory-write fail intermittently. Fixed by warming the module graph in beforeAll, not by raising the timeout. Left alone deliberately: two instances were fixed and verified before generalising. Separately, this file mocks PrismaClient BY PROTOTYPE, so any new prisma.* call falls through to the real client and fails silently.

items.title: Unattributed: tinypool 'Worker exited unexpectedly' in the webhook suite
items.gap: Observed once in three full runs, losing one file's 3 tests. A process exit, not an assertion failure; did not recur. A 1-in-3 intermittent cannot be attributed by a single baseline run, so it is recorded rather than explained. A second, distinct flake mechanism from the timeout one already fixed.

items.title: ARETE_MCP_TOKEN_KEY is undocumented in .env.example
items.gap: Left undone on purpose because .env.example belonged to another lane, and now neither lane owns it — a live orphan. Generator: python -c "from arete_agents.mcp.token_crypto import generate_key; print(generate_key())". Without the key, the store silently keeps working in plaintext with a single warning.

items.title: Sensorium vitality / necrosis / heat-churn sensors
items.gap: get_architecture v0.8.1 exposes no TESTS edge and no dead-code flag, so the untested and dead-code sensors are honestly absent in v1 rather than zeroed to look real — joining the already-deferred heat/churn sensor. Needs a newer indexer capability or a different data source.
blockedBy: ext:upstream indexer must expose TESTS edges and dead-code flags

items.title: The live MCP stdio session cannot complete initialize
items.gap: The codebase-memory-mcp binary writes level=… logs to STDOUT, corrupting the JSON-RPC stream; CLI mode works. Consequence: the live-session → HTTP available path and the OAuth-gated signed-in render were never driven. Flagged for a PM integration drive on 2026-07-15 and resolved by no later document.
blockedBy: ext:needs a binary build that logs to stderr, or a deployed environment

items.title: framer-motion → motion migration (and LazyMotion)
items.gap: framer-motion is deprecated and renamed, with an identical API at the same version — an import rewrite across 19 files. Adopting LazyMotion + m would take the shell from a 34 kb eager bundle to about 4.6 kb with domMax loaded async.

items.title: Evaluate Vercel AI Elements for the agent UI
items.gap: Under evaluation, not committed. Ships exactly the interaction logic the investigations surface needs (Tool with four states, ChainOfThought, Reasoning, Task, InlineCitation), vendored as source so the logic can be taken and the skin discarded. Wants shadcn/ui initialised and Tailwind in CSS-variables mode, whereas six primitives are hand-rolled today. The decision was deferred to an implementation plan that was never written.

items.title: Make the Kuma logo a true global refresh
items.gap: The fake 3-second simulate-load spinner was removed. The proposal is a real re-pull of services, connections, telemetry and scan state with per-source results, where a failing source must say so — a spin that resolves on a timer would be exactly the fabricated status the honesty rules forbid.

items.title: Blind reloads lose the user's place
items.gap: Scan uses setTimeout(reload, 1500) unconnected to actual completion, and Fix/Dismiss use window.location.reload(), losing rail expansion, selection and stream position. router.refresh() already exists in this codebase as the better-behaved sibling.

items.title: Two links point somewhere they should not
items.gap: "Back to Overview" points at / (the marketing root) in reviews/[id]/page.tsx:96. "Explore with sample data →" links to /overview, which never shows sample data — and promising sample data that does not exist is a small fabrication.

items.title: Adopt getAccountState on the last two ad-hoc pages
items.gap: agents/page.tsx and map/page.tsx are the last two ad-hoc hasAccess users, so they can drift from the account-state contract independently of every other surface.

items.title: Review.prUrl + resolve/noise reason codes
items.gap: Only prNumber is stored, so nothing can link straight to the PR, and resolutions carry no reason code to learn from.

items.title: Self-host + enterprise packaging
items.gap: Deferred by design. Listed so it is not mistaken for forgotten.

items.title: Decide the fate of the TYME platform proposal
items.gap: 35 KB of founding product vision (the Master Grid, business-to-code traceability, the OODA loop) referenced by no status doc, roadmap, backlog entry or coordination claim. Either it still describes where this is going and should be linked from the roadmap, or it is superseded and should say so. Right now it is neither.

items.title: Give every worktree its own database
items.gap: All worktrees share one Postgres, and `prisma db push --accept-data-loss` has clobbered other checkouts three times in one day — dropping ModelConnection.userId and the whole ErrorGroup table. pyrosome has its own database and uses migrate deploy; horseshoe and ridley still run db push against the shared one. This is live data loss waiting to happen again, and the fix is already proven in one worktree.

items.title: Google sign-in is dead — the OAuth client no longer exists
items.gap: Returns 401 invalid_client because the client was deleted upstream. Either provision a new client or remove the button — a sign-in option that always fails is worse than one that is absent.
blockedBy: ext:a new Google OAuth client must be provisioned

items.title: Make the Incident Signals panel visibly render
items.gap: The panel and the tenant-scoped query surface are shipped, but showing them working needs a real Alertmanager alert to trip an error-rate or p95 threshold. Per the no-synthetic-seeds rule none was fabricated, so the demo waits on a real incident.
blockedBy: ext:needs a real Alertmanager alert to fire

items.title: Is haiku adequate for fix authoring?
items.gap: Carried out of Phase 3 and never settled. Needs a regression corpus and a funded API key to measure against; the account is at zero.
blockedBy: ext:needs a funded Anthropic key and a regression corpus

items.title: Four typed-debt ratchets are held at warn
items.gap: webhook no-explicit-any is off; agents ruff line-length is 120; the dashboard holds 51 anys at warn; webhook holds 78 no-console at warn. Each is a deliberate ratchet, and a ratchet nobody tightens is just debt with better manners.

items.title: ClickHouse DDL reads as self-contradictory on TTLs
items.gap: Two views declare an inline 30d TTL that a later statement overrides to 90d. The end state is correct; the file just reads as contradictory to anyone auditing retention. Two other tables also lack ttl_only_drop_parts.
